title: ocotea corrugata
intro: Ocotea corrugata, also known as the West Indian sandalwood, is a species of evergreen tree native to Central and South America. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its rich content of sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Studies have shown that Ocotea corrugata has anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce stress, improve sleep quality, and boost the immune system. Ocotea corrugata can be prepared as a tea, oil, or capsule, and is often used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety.
